portfolio_view: These five span Telecom, Oil & Gas, Pharma, Capital Goods, and PSU Banking — no sector overlap, and archetypes range from confirmed turnarounds (STLTECH, CHENNPETRO, EMCURE) to a clean structural-theme quality name (KEI) and a deep-value re-rating candidate (MAHABANK). Caveat: two of the turnaround names (STLTECH, EMCURE) now carry stretched valuations after sharp re-ratings — size these with the stretched multiple in mind, not just the story.

meta.highlights: governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); earnings 83: qtr PAT YoY +25%; accelerating (TTM 32% > 3y 24%); technicals/RS 76: RS percentile 78; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; theme 65: themes: data centre (news-based v0)

picks.selected_because: Balanced 68.0 score with clean technicals (RS 78, stage-2) and a confirmed structural theme (data-centre cabling) rather than a one-quarter spike — best-diversified Capital Goods entry (avoids PSU/governance overlaps elsewhere in that bucket).
picks.thesis: Management is guiding >20% revenue growth for Q1 and FY27, driven by export recovery and a scaling data-centre cable business (currently ₹400-500cr base, seen as a multi-year opportunity) — a genuine infra/digitization tailwind.
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results confirming the 20% guidance.
picks.management: Clean governance, no pledge, long execution track record.
picks.risks: Copper/aluminium input-cost volatility; rising competition from Polycab/RR Kabel in the same niche.
picks.watch_for: Guidance downgrade or margin compression from input costs.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 6.74->11.47%; DII 11.33->10.84%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); earnings 91: loss->profit swing CONFIRMED by margin expansion (OPM 12.0->14.0%); accelerating (TTM 138%; technicals/RS 85: RS percentile 100; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Highest conviction (72.6) and perfect RS (100); mechanical earnings-turnaround flag is real — FY26 profit swing confirmed with margin expansion — and smart money (FII 6.7%→11.5%) is corroborating it.
picks.thesis: Q4 FY26 net profit was ₹59cr vs a ₹17cr loss prior quarter; FY26 revenue +18.8% to ₹4,745cr; order book up 67% YoY to ₹7,309cr, driven by AI/data-centre optical-fiber demand.
picks.catalyst: FY27 order-book execution and recent QIP-funded capacity.
picks.management: No pledge disclosed; QIP has strengthened the balance sheet post-restructuring.
picks.risks: Trades at ~266x P/E, 27x EV/EBITDA, debt/EBITDA 5.81x, ROE near zero — priced for flawless execution after a 359% one-year run.
picks.watch_for: Any order-book or margin miss given how little room the valuation leaves.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 8.8->14.98%; DII 2.6->1.01%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); balance-sheet 91: deleveraging: debt 4260 -> 1964 Cr; earnings 91: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 1349% > 3y -4%); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: 72.1 score with strongest balance-sheet dimension (debt cut 4260→1964cr) plus a genuine new catalyst research uncovered — Navratna status granted June 2026, unlocking capex autonomy; Oil & Gas/PSU adds sector diversification.
picks.thesis: GRM recovered to $9.28/bbl (FY26) from $4.22/bbl, driving Q4 PAT to ₹1,421.85cr vs ₹469.93cr YoY; Navratna status should speed refinery/petrochemical capex approvals.
picks.catalyst: Navratna-enabled capex announcements; current favorable refining cycle.
picks.management: PSU-promoted, no pledge, credible deleveraging execution.
picks.risks: Earnings are GRM/crude-cycle driven, not structural — CPCL posted an actual loss in Q1 FY26 before the margin recovery; this is a cyclical, not secular, story.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 2.86->3.35%; DII 2.84->6.1%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); balance-sheet 87: deleveraging: debt 2334 -> 1558 Cr; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); earnings 82: qtr PAT YoY +24%; accelerating (TTM 38% > 3y 21%)

picks.selected_because: 69.5 score, standout smart-money dimension (DII stake more than doubled 2.84%→6.1%), and real deleveraging (debt 2334→1558cr); Healthcare adds a defensive, less-cyclical sleeve versus the industrial/PSU names above.
picks.thesis: Q4 FY26 PAT +23.6% YoY on steady double-digit growth; deleveraging is funding margin expansion, and the board's FY26 dividend approval signals confidence in cash generation from the formulations/biosimilars business.
picks.catalyst: Dividend follow-through and continued deleveraging into FY27.
picks.management: Promoter holding steady (~78%), no pledge; rising DII conviction is a genuine positive signal.
picks.risks: Rich valuation (TTM P/E ~34.7x vs Cipla 23x, Dr Reddy's 17x) against a modest ~9% 5-yr sales CAGR; FII actually trimmed its stake QoQ (3.59%→3.35%) — a mild contra-signal to the DII buying.
picks.watch_for: Any margin disappointment that fails to justify the premium multiple.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 1.89->5.55%; DII 10.4->13.93%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 97: RS percentile 93; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; valuation 95: P/E 10 cheaper than growth (PEG 0.25); earnings 62: qtr PAT YoY +36%; decelerating (TTM 27% < 3y 39%)

picks.selected_because: Standout valuation dimension (P/E 10, PEG 0.25 — cheapest re-rating case on the shortlist) with strong technicals (RS 93, live VCP) and rising smart-money interest; PSU banking is a genuine diversifier against the growth/turnaround-heavy rest of the portfolio.
picks.thesis: 19-22% YoY business/advance growth with 27-36% PAT growth, trading far below private-bank multiples — a classic PSU re-rating candidate as clean asset quality gets recognized.
picks.catalyst: None hugely positive near-term; note the government's planned ~6% OFS (to meet the Aug-2026 minimum-public-shareholding deadline) is a known supply event, not a re-rating trigger.
picks.management: GOI promoter (73.6%), no pledge, asset-quality repair largely complete.
picks.risks: Mechanical data already flags earnings deceleration (TTM 27% < 3yr 39%); the pending GOI OFS/QIP creates a real near-term share-supply overhang.
picks.watch_for: OFS pricing/timing and whether earnings growth stabilizes or keeps decelerating.
